transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hi, how's it going?
It's okay.
I'm feeling a lot better.
Camera off because I'm not looking the greatest.
But I'm feeling a lot better than yesterday.
Yesterday was , actually.
Are you just like, do you have a cold?
Does it just, you also have a fever?
I didn't have a fever, thankfully, but I was just feeling like, you know, when anything that you touch or when you touch yourself, it really hurts.
Like my throat is really painful and congestion and I'm feeling better today.
Not a hundred percent, like I'm not going to go out and about and do normal, my normal like fitness or whatever, but definitely a lot better mentally.
Like my brain's actually working, which is great.
That's good.
I got sick when I was on vacation.
No!
Were you in Greece?

Yeah, I got sick like halfway through and I always make this mistake and it's like, I cannot, if I'm laying out in the sun or if it's just hot and I have like a drink or two that's really cold, like something happens, I always get a cold.
Like my nose just like stops working.
Yeah.
I don't know.
it's just that.
just...

I think I was just probably like, just like, you know, when you're like, relaxed, and like, your cortisol levels drop, and then all of a sudden, like, your immune system's got like, okay, it's time to get sick.
Yeah, it's time, they caught you slipping.
Exactly.
Yeah, I feel like temperature changes affect me as well.
And it might be because I was in the sun a lot on Sunday.
I don't know.
Like, when it gets from really hot to, like, colder here, I notice that I start to feel like, like, not as sick as I was yesterday, but I'm feeling like, not, not good at all.
So.
Yeah, yeah, got it.
But yeah, thank you for helping me edit the last two articles.
I know that Sarah's picking on some more.
We just need to get more ideas, at least two more ideas of truth.
And she's, she's working on three right now.
Okay, okay.
Sounds good.
Okay, cool.
I think, yeah, I sent the three this morning, and then, yeah, I think we should be able to, like, start publishing as soon as the blog is up on their site.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Hi.
Hi.
Hi.
I've been just like, late last week, and this week's been like pretty, just like my, my cat, not the cat that I, that's living with me in my apartment.
Yeah, I totally forgot, how's your cat?

Um, well, they held him, like, over the weekend, his, like, kidney, like, they did his blood work, and the kidney parameters were pretty high, um, so they left him over the weekend to, like, give him an IV, basically every day, to kind of, like, flush out his kidneys, um, which worked, but they still want to give him, like, subcutaneous injections.

Like, he has to go back in tomorrow, Friday, and then three days next week, um, just to get, like, it's kind of like, uh, it's similar to an IV, but it releases over time, um, but yeah, he isn't, like, like, his kidneys are kind of.

Not have kind of, you know, have like lower function, which is just, yeah, like, and my mom, the worst thing is that my mom's like away, before she's in Germany right now, she's working, and like, it's just kind of hard for her as well, like not being there, because, you know, he's a baby.
Yeah, oh my god, Aida, I'm sorry.

Yeah, he's a, yeah, but he's like doing, I don't know, he was a, the vet, he really like loves like animals, like I had, like he was sending me WhatsApp updates, and like photos, like every time he would go in, and like, like he would take pictures, and tell me that he was eating, and stuff like that, so, at least the vet, like really, he really cares about the animals, which is nice.
I love that, yeah, oh my god, I can't imagine like, you work with animals, like there's, must be people who work with animals, who like.
Don't care as much.
That makes me sad.
yeah.
I mean, they are in, like, they live in a pretty, like, rural area.
So they are just, like, the first vet that we went to, just to, like, get his, like, Croatian passport, because they moved to Croatian last year.
Like, it was pretty, like.
You need animal passports?
Yeah, like, like, for one day, like, the European Union has, like, a pet passport.
Oh, that's cool.
Yeah, maybe you don't have them.
Do you not have them?
I don't know.
I mean, I don't, I've never owned a pet that I had to, like, take out of the country.
So it's possible that it exists, and I just don't know.
Yeah, yeah, I think it's a thing.
It's just that, like, the U.S.
is so big, you probably don't need to take, like, wherever you plan on going with the cat, it's probably in the U.S., so you don't need to pass.
That's true.
I think, yeah, but I'm looking it up, and there are.
Actually, some required documents for international travel.
It's like vaccines and stuff.
Yeah, yeah.
makes sense.
Yeah, that's basically the pet passport.
It's like a picture of the pet, their name, and then like the vaccines.
Oh, okay, yeah.
Yeah, because I know a lot of people who travel with their pets, but it's usually within the country, so.
Yeah, yeah.
I mean, the vet that we first took him to, he was a little bit like, I don't know.
It was very like a country vet.
Like, they work with, like, cows, and I don't know.
They're just used to seeing animals as, like, just livestock.
So they don't really, they aren't really too kind.
Yeah.
it was great.
Like, they, I saw on their Facebook page that someone brought them a hedgehog that they saw was, like, run over by a car.
But the hedgehog was fine.
It was just, like, bleeding a little bit.
And he, like, came in the middle of the night to treat the hedgehog, and it's fine.
Aww.
Aww.
Aww.
Oh, my God.
A few weeks ago, I was out for dinner with my family and my boyfriend and we were like after dinner going to the parking lot to like leave and go home.
And then there was a dog in the parking lot and I was like, oh my God, how cute.
Just like a stray dog.
And the dog saw us and got scared and she ran.
And she just like freaked out and ran to the parking lot on the street.
And it's like a fairly busy street.
This is not a horrible story.
I'm not going to tell you like this horrible traumatic story.
She's fine.
But we saw her run to the street and she got hit by a car because it happened so fast.
They couldn't see her.
And she was like in the street.
My boyfriend like runs into the street and like picks her up and holds her.
And he's like, we need to get her to the emergency vet.
And I'm like freaking out.
And the girl who had driven the car had like pulled over and she was like a teenager, like a young girl.
And she was like, it was like traumatic for her.
And so we just like made sure.
There was this runner who had seen something happen and, like, came over and was, like, asking us or, like, trying to help us.
And I found, thankfully, there was, like, an animal shelter very close by that has, like, a 24-7 emergency vet that we were able to drop them off at.
And so once that was figured out, I, like, checked on the girl who was driving.
She was crying.
She was, like, traumatized by this.
was, like, there's nothing you can do.
She's okay.
Like, she's not paralyzed or, like, traumatically injured or anything like that, the dog.
And I'm, like, please don't, like, feel bad.
Yeah.
And, like, making sure that she was not, like, you know, too traumatized by this experience.
And then we, like, took the dog to the shelter.
And eventually, like, she didn't have any sort of chip.
She wasn't fixed.
She didn't have any owner.
But she was, like, a year and a half old.
And she didn't look like she had been living on the streets for that long.
Like, she looked healthy and not, like, roughed up or super skinny or anything like that.
So I didn't know what ended up happening to her.
Because the shelter was...
If take dogs in, or any animals in, they don't give out information.
I don't know why.
I think maybe it's just like they have so many animals that they have to take care of, they can't keep on top of random people who want information about animals under their care.
But yeah, I would keep track of her little profile page on their website, and eventually she was listed as available for adoption, and then very quickly after, her page was taken down.
So maybe her family found her, someone adopted her.
But either way, I think it's a good story.
Yeah, I it.
It was a good story.
That's good.
Yeah, yeah.
It's nice to hear.
Yeah, it's just crazy to witness.
Like, never seen an animal, like, get injured like that right in front of me.
Yeah, I've never, I had, yeah, it happens.
Like, I, one time I almost got in a car crash, not because I was, like, driving home to visit my parents.
And there's, like, the stretch of the road through Bosnia that, like, goes through.

Not mountains, but it's a pretty like, you know, like the roads are like super curvy and the dog ran out in the road and I think there were like three or four cars in front of me and like the first driver, he braked really hard and I kind of hit the car in front of me, but it wasn't too bad.
Like I got out of the car and there was virtually like no damage and on the sweet old man's car.
And I looked at my car and he was kind of looking at me and I shook his hand.
I'm like, we're good.
Like, let's just keep it moving.
Like we're not going to hold up traffic over nothing really.
So, but yeah, it does happen a lot.
But yeah, I mean, I think especially, yeah, there's just stray animals living all around us and we don't even know until.
Yeah, there used to be a lot of stray dogs in Sarajevo a few years ago.
Um, when I, but not a few years ago, like, 10 years ago.
Um, and then I think they started, like, like, these, like, mass, like, spay and neuter programs.
So now it's not, like, it's pretty rare to see stray dogs, at least, like, in this city, but there's a lot of, like, stray cats, which, like, cats just reproduce at, like, alarming rates.
Yeah, especially if they're not thick.
Yeah.
Because I think they can, like, start having babies when they're less than a year old.
And then those babies will have babies.
It's just crazy.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Mice are pretty crazy in that respect.
Like, mice, they just multiply.
We had, like, we had a situation.
I was visiting my boyfriend.
This was early this year.
Um, and they have a house.
And there's, uh, they, it was, the weather was nice.
They always keep the door open.
I remember it was the middle of the night.
I woke up because I felt him get up and go to the bathroom.
He came back in the room.
I could just sense him standing in the middle of the room and kind of looking at the floor.
I was like, what's wrong with you?
What's going on?
He said, I think there's a mouse in this room.
He was just standing there and I was frozen.
All of a sudden, I heard this across the ground.
Oh my God.
He's never been in the same room with a mouse.
So I went downstairs and he was just trying to get it out.
So the mouse did get out and they were able to like get it out of the house.
But I was just telling him like, listen, if you have mice anywhere in your house, it's going to be a huge problem because they do like reproduce.
There's never just one.
Yeah.
Like it's not just one or two.
It's going to be a huge problem.
If Drew ever watches this recording.
I just learned a lot about the pets.
I just learned a lot about animals.
I know.
I don't know.
I did send him a follow-up message in Slack, but we could just send an async update.
Okay, yeah.
Okay, cool.
I guess we should give you back your time.
have a lot to catch up on.
Okay, all right.
All right, I'll see you.
Imagine he joins right now.
But it'll be fine.
All right.
Thanks, Aida.
All right.
See ya, bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[A check-in on content progress and a personal catch-up.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=39.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Blocked:** Publishing of 3 completed articles is paused pending the new blog's launch.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** Sarah is drafting 3 new articles, but 2 more ideas are needed to fill the queue.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**Personal Updates:** Aida's cat is recovering from kidney issues, and Nathalie is recovering from a cold.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Publishing

  - [**Completed:** Aida edited 2 articles; 3 more were sent for review.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**Blocker:** Publishing is on hold until the new blog is live.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=182.0)
  - [**In Progress:** Sarah is drafting 3 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**Need:** 2 additional article ideas to maintain the content pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)

### Personal Updates

  - [**Aida's Cat:**](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)
      - [**Issue:** High kidney parameters.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)
      - [**Treatment:** Daily weekend IVs to flush kidneys, followed by subcutaneous injections on Wed/Fri and 3 days next week.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)
  - [**Nathalie's Health:**](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=42.0)
      - [Recovering from a cold with a sore throat and congestion.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=57.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:** Send an async update to Drew via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
  - [**Nathalie:** Generate 2 new article ideas.](https://fathom.video/share/cf_4dNGsk8xmUx5PSDLoYnw9yWmD1ZxW?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
